_catalog.name: CargoTrack Dangerous Goods Intelligence Catalog
sources: ADR 2023 (European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road)
ICAO Technical Instructions for the Safe Transport of Dangerous Goods by Air
UN Model Regulations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods (23rd revised edition)
_catalog.disclaimer: This catalog is for risk screening and awareness purposes. Always consult the current edition of applicable regulations (IATA DGR, IMDG Code, ADR) before shipping dangerous goods. Regulations change annually.

classes.label: Explosives
requiredDocuments: Shipper's Declaration for Dangerous Goods (ICAO/IATA format)
UN Number and Proper Shipping Name on outer packaging
classes.airFreightNotes: Class 1 Explosives (most divisions) FORBIDDEN on passenger and cargo aircraft. 1.4S limited exceptions exist.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 1. Strict stowage and segregation requirements. Advance notification to port authority required.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 1. Tunnel restrictions apply. Distance from populated areas regulations.
classes.packagingRequirements: UN-approved packaging mandatory. Strict quantity limits per package. Compatibility between different subdivisions must be verified.
classes.screeningNotes: Explosives require government licenses in most jurisdictions. High diversion risk. Extra due diligence on end-user required.

classes.label: Gases
classes.airFreightNotes: Class 2.1 Flammable gases: most FORBIDDEN by air. 2.2 Non-flammable gases: allowed with restrictions. 2.3 Toxic gases: FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 2. Closed cargo areas required for flammable gases. Temperature control for some.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 2. Specific tank requirements for pressurized gases.
classes.packagingRequirements: Pressure-rated cylinders or tanks. UN-certified packaging. Valves must be protected.
classes.screeningNotes: Toxic gases (2.3) require special carrier approval and government permits in many jurisdictions.

classes.label: Flammable Liquids
requiredDocuments: UN Number and Packing Group on label
classes.airFreightNotes: Class 3 Packing Group I (PG I — very high danger): FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft. PG II/III: allowed with quantity limits. Some articles (perfumes <2L) permitted as consumer commodities.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 3. Stowage categories A-D depending on flash point and packing group.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 3. Tank or packaged. Specific vehicle requirements for bulk.
classes.packagingRequirements: UN-certified containers. Packing Group determines performance level required. Maximum quantity limits per package.
classes.screeningNotes: Very common. Risk screening should focus on quantity (bulk shipments indicate industrial rather than consumer use), flash point, and packing group.

classes.label: Flammable Solids
subclasses: 4.1 Flammable solids, self-reactive substances, desensitized explosives
4.3 Substances which emit flammable gases on contact with water
classes.airFreightNotes: Class 4.1: many restricted by air; self-reactive substances (4.1 Type B and above) often FORBIDDEN. Class 4.2: most FORBIDDEN by passenger aircraft. Class 4.3: restrictions vary.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 4. Water-reactive substances require dry stowage.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 4. Special handling for water-reactive materials.
classes.packagingRequirements: UN-certified. Inner packaging required for water-reactive substances. No moisture exposure.
classes.screeningNotes: Class 4.3 (water-reactive) particularly hazardous — look for sodium, lithium, calcium carbide in descriptions.

classes.label: Oxidizing Substances and Organic Peroxides
classes.iataLabel: Class 5 — Oxidizing Substances and Organic Peroxides
classes.imdgLabel: Class 5 — Oxidizing Substances and Organic Peroxides
classes.airFreightNotes: Ammonium nitrate: FORBIDDEN by air. Organic peroxides (5.2 Type A-B): FORBIDDEN by air. Types C-F: restricted.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 5. Ammonium nitrate: special stowage away from heat sources. Segregation from flammables.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 5. Ammonium nitrate fertilizer has specific ADR regulations.
classes.packagingRequirements: UN-certified. Organic peroxides require temperature control during transport.
classes.screeningNotes: High dual-use risk for ammonium nitrate (fertilizer/explosive). IED precursor. Enhanced due diligence on buyer.

classes.label: Toxic and Infectious Substances
requiredDocuments: Permis d'importation (for Category A infectious substances in some countries)
UN Number (UN2814 Cat A, UN3373 Cat B)
classes.airFreightNotes: Category A Infectious Substances (UN2814/UN2900): allowed with strict P602 packaging. Category B (UN3373): P650 packaging. Toxic substances: restrictions vary by toxicity.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 6. Category A: closed containers. Segregation from food and feed.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 6. Category A: specialized transport with biohazard placarding.
classes.packagingRequirements: Infectious substances: triple packaging (primary, secondary, rigid outer). Primary must be leak-proof.
classes.screeningNotes: Biological Category A substances require prior government authorization in most countries. Cyanide compounds have dual-use and diversion risk.

classes.label: Radioactive Material
requiredDocuments: Competent Authority Approval (for Type B and C packages, fissile material)
Criticality Safety Index (CSI) for fissile material
Multilateral or bilateral government approval as required
classes.airFreightNotes: Radioactive material: allowed by air with strict conditions. Fissile material requires Competent Authority Approval. High TI materials require exclusive use of aircraft.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 7. Advance notification to port required. Vessel master notification mandatory.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 7. Route planning required. Escort may be required for high-activity sources.
classes.packagingRequirements: UN-certified Type A, B, or C packages (activity-dependent). Radiation shielding integral to package design.
classes.screeningNotes: HIGHEST RISK — dual-use and WMD concern. Government authorization mandatory. Screen against nuclear proliferators. Any irregularity must be escalated.

classes.label: Corrosive Substances
requiredDocuments: UN Number and Packing Group on label
classes.airFreightNotes: Strong acids and alkalis restricted; PG I FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft. Batteries (wet): FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft cargo hold. Certain quantities allowed as carry-on.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 8. Segregation from foodstuffs, animal feeding stuffs. Acid/alkali segregation.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 8. Chemical-resistant tanks for bulk.
classes.packagingRequirements: Chemical-resistant inner packaging. Absorbent material in outer packaging for liquids. Tightly closed to prevent leakage.
classes.screeningNotes: Nitric acid is an explosive precursor — dual-use concern. Mercury restricted or banned for import in many jurisdictions (Minamata Convention).

classes.label: Miscellaneous Dangerous Goods
requiredDocuments: Shipper's Declaration for Dangerous Goods (lithium batteries exceeding limits)
State of Charge documentation (lithium batteries must be ≤30% SOC for damaged/defective)
Quantity limits per package compliance documentation
classes.airFreightNotes: Lithium-ion batteries (UN3480): FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft as cargo if standalone cells >160 Wh or >2 batteries. Equipment-installed batteries: restricted. Spare batteries: strict limits in carry-on. EV lithium batteries: complex separate rules.
classes.seaFreightNotes: IMDG Class 9. Lithium batteries require special stowage. Asbestos: strictly controlled — most types banned for import in EU, UK, Australia.
classes.roadFreightNotes: ADR Class 9. Lithium batteries: placarding and documentation required for large quantities.
classes.packagingRequirements: Lithium batteries: cells protected against short circuit, inner packaging, quantity limits per package. State of charge ≤30% for recalled/damaged.
classes.screeningNotes: Lithium batteries are the most common DG compliance issue in air freight. Undeclared batteries in electronics shipments is a frequent violation. Check all electronics shipments.

typesCovered: UN3481 Lithium ion batteries in equipment
UN3091 Lithium metal batteries in equipment

keyLimits.cellCapacity_Wh: Cells >20 Wh or batteries >100 Wh require Section II declarations. Cells >60 Wh or batteries >300 Wh may require Section I.
keyLimits.SOC_regulation: State of charge must not exceed 30% for damaged/defective cells. Standard shipment: ≤30% SOC recommended for air.
keyLimits.passengerAircraftLimit: Standalone lithium-ion cells >160 Wh or batteries >2 per package: FORBIDDEN on passenger aircraft cargo

hsCodeDGMapping.28: Class 2, 5, 6, 7, 8 (inorganic chemicals — acids, gases, radioactive, toxic)
hsCodeDGMapping.29: Class 3, 6, 8 (organic chemicals — solvents, toxic compounds, corrosives)
hsCodeDGMapping.30: Class 6 (pharmaceuticals — may include biologics Category A/B)
hsCodeDGMapping.31: Class 5.1 (fertilizers — ammonium nitrate dual-use explosive precursor)
hsCodeDGMapping.39: Class 3 (polymers in primary forms — some flammable)
hsCodeDGMapping.71: Class 7 (radioactive isotopes — precious metals sector may include pitchblende/uranium minerals)
hsCodeDGMapping.87: Class 3, 9 (vehicles — fuel tanks, lithium batteries in EVs)
hsCodeDGMapping.90: Class 6, 7 (medical devices — may include radioactive sources, biological)
